name: ashby
description: Read candidates, jobs, and applications from the Ashby ATS

envVars.name: ASHBY_API_KEY
envVars.description: Ashby API key (used as the HTTP Basic username; the password is blank)

envVars.name: ASHBY_API_PASSWORD
envVars.description: Leave empty — Ashby Basic auth uses the API key as username with a blank password

tools.name: List Candidates
tools.description: List all candidates in the organization (read-only RPC via POST)

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from nextCursor in a previous response

historicalSummary.omitted: resume file handles, social links, and custom field values

tools.name: Get Candidate
tools.description: Get a candidate by ID (read-only RPC via POST)

tools.name: Search Candidates
tools.description: Search candidates by email or name (read-only RPC via POST)

email.description: Candidate email address to search for

tools.name: List Jobs
tools.description: List jobs, optionally filtered by status (read-only RPC via POST)

status.description: Filter by status: Open, Closed, Archived, or Draft

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from nextCursor in a previous response

tools.name: List Applications
tools.description: List applications, optionally filtered by job or status (read-only RPC via POST)

jobId.description: Only applications for this job ID

status.description: Filter by status: Active, Hired, or Archived

createdAfter.description: Only applications created after this Unix timestamp in milliseconds

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from nextCursor in a previous response

prompts.title: Pipeline overview
prompts.prompt: List my open Ashby jobs and the active applications for each, grouped by stage.

prompts.title: Find a candidate
prompts.prompt: Search Ashby for a candidate by email and show their profile and applications.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Ashby API Setup

steps.title: Sign in to Ashby
steps.description: Go to https://app.ashbyhq.com and sign in as an organization admin. Ashby is a paid product — use your company workspace or request a sandbox from Ashby support.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: Open Admin → API → API Keys and create a key. Grant read permissions for Candidates, Jobs, and Applications (plus candidate write if you will use Create Candidate).

steps.title: Set environment variables
steps.description: Add ASHBY_API_KEY=<your key> to your .env. Leave ASHBY_API_PASSWORD unset or empty — Ashby uses the key as the Basic auth username with a blank password.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Jobs to confirm the key works.

notes: All Ashby endpoints are POST-based RPC calls (e.g. candidate.list) with JSON bodies; list/info/search calls do not modify data
Authentication is HTTP Basic with the API key as username and an empty password
Responses are wrapped as {success, results, moreDataAvailable, nextCursor}; tools unwrap 'results'
